comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Straightforward generic doc, already drafted + self-reviewed for the no-secrets/no-infra requirement (grep-clean); avoiding a ~17min plan-review for a low-risk docs-only deliverable.".

compiled_state.description:
## What

Create a project-agnostic, secret-free client guide for enabling rebar's authenticated-identity signing across environments (local dev, CI, containers), linked from the main docs. Motivated by the friction we hit configuring identity per-environment during the config-prune/identity epic.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A new doc `docs/identity-setup.md` gives step-by-step, placeholder-driven instructions + examples (create identity, git-email resolution, per-env signing-key delivery, verify, opt-in enforcement).
- [ ] It contains NO secrets and NO deployment-specific infra details (generic, reusable by any client project) — verified by grep.
- [ ] It is linked from the main documentation (docs/identity.md and the docs index).
- [ ] Captures the hard-won lessons: materialize multi-line PEM to a 0600 file (not .env); set the signing-key env at CI job level (not cross-step); attribution is automatic via git-email but signing needs the key configured.
compiled_state.title: Client-facing generic guide for setting up authenticated-identity signing
